summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the flatted parse() function allows prototype pollution via untrusted JSON input containing __proto__ property keys. After parsing malicious input, all JavaScript objects inherit the polluted property.
expected_security_impact: Parsing a crafted flatted JSON string containing __proto__ property should pollute Object.prototype, causing all subsequently created JavaScript objects to inherit the malicious property, demonstrating successful prototype pollution.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched a Node.js container, executed the exploit script that parsed malicious JSON with __proto__ property, and verified that Object.prototype was polluted, as shown by {}.polluted === 'yes' returning true for all new objects.
verification_method: The exploit script (1) parses malicious flatted JSON containing __proto__ property, (2) creates a new empty object {}, (3) checks if the empty object has the polluted property, and (4) exits with success code if pollution is confirmed. This distinguishes prototype pollution from normal property assignment.
evidence_produced_by_run: The parsed JSON successfully polluted Object.prototype.
Empty objects created after parsing inherited the malicious property: {}.polluted === 'yes'.
All objects in the JavaScript environment are affected, demonstrating the severity of prototype pollution.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
